name: Ascent of Mont-Louis and col de la Llose
name_original: Montée de Mont-Louis et col de la Llose
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Souanyas offer access to diverse and challenging terrain within the Pyrénées-Orientales department of southern France. The region is characterized by winding valley roads, demanding mountain passes, and views of the Canigó massif. Situated in the Conflent region, Souanyas provides a base for exploring routes with significant elevation gains along the Têt river valley. The landscape combines Mediterranean and mountain influences, featuring gorges and ancient massifs. Best road cycling routes around Souanyas The most popular road cycling route is Ascent of Mont-Louis and col de la Llose, a 31.8 miles (51.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant climbs over mountain passes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Col de la Creu – Col de la Llose loop from Olette, a difficult 43.6 miles (70.2 km) path. This loop navigates through multiple mountain passes, offering sustained ascents and descents. Local road cyclists also love the Beautiful mountain road – Col de la Llose loop from Serdinya, a 51.6 miles (83.0 km) trail leading through scenic mountain roads, often completed in about 4 hours 46 minutes. Road cycling around Souanyas is defined by challenging mountain passes, winding valley roads, and views of the Canigó massif. The network offers options for difficult routes with substantial elevation gains, alongside some moderate and easy trails. The routes in Souanyas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Souanyas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How challenging is road cycling around Souanyas?
A: Road cycling around Souanyas is primarily characterized by challenging mountain passes and significant elevation gains. The region, nestled in the Pyrénées-Orientales, offers winding valley roads and demanding climbs, with routes often featuring sustained ascents and descents. While there are some moderate and easy options, the area is best known for its difficult routes, making it ideal for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Souanyas?
A: You can expect a diverse and challenging terrain. Routes often traverse winding valley roads, demanding mountain passes, and dramatic landscapes such as gorges. The area is influenced by both Mediterranean and mountain climates, offering a mix of natural beauty along the Têt river valley and views of the iconic Canigó massif. The roads provide varied lengths and gradients, catering to different cycling abilities.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Souanyas area?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Souanyas on komoot. These routes cater to various difficulty levels, with a significant number of challenging options that include substantial elevation gains.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural landmarks to see while road cycling near Souanyas?
A: Yes, the region offers numerous scenic viewpoints and natural landmarks. You can enjoy routes with inspiring views of the Canigó massif. Notable natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/886638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic de la Pena&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4534307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roc de la Foradada&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3109209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col Mitjà&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, you might encounter a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7020248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small Waterfall in Parc Naturel Régional des Pyrénées Catalanes&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7031291" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Hot Spring of Thuès-les-Bains&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Souanyas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain passes, the breathtaking views of the Canigó massif, and the diverse terrain that makes for rewarding rides. The sense of accomplishment after tackling routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949411277/ascent-of-mont-louis-and-col-de-la-llose" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent of Mont-Louis and col de la Llose&lt;/a&gt; is frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any loop trails for road cycling around Souanyas?
A: Yes, there are several excellent loop trails for road cycling. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36281984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de la Creu – Col de la Llose loop from Olette&lt;/a&gt;, which navigates through multiple mountain passes. Another highly-rated loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36220338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful mountain road – Col de la Llose loop from Serdinya&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic mountain roads.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Souanyas?
A: The Pyrénées-Orientales region, where Souanyas is located, generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling from spring through autumn. The summer months provide warm weather, while spring and early autumn offer milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Cyclists should be prepared for varying mountain weather conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes near Souanyas?
A: While many routes around Souanyas feature significant climbs and are rated as difficult, there are some easier options available. The region's valley roads along the Têt river can offer less strenuous sections suitable for families, though specific dedicated family-friendly routes might require exploring options in flatter areas or regional parks.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the road cycling routes in Souanyas?
A: Souanyas itself is a small commune, but its strategic location means you can find amenities in nearby villages and towns within the Conflent region. These areas offer cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options that cater to visitors, including cyclists. It's advisable to plan your stops in advance, especially on longer routes, to ensure you have access to refreshments.

Q: Is public transport available to reach Souanyas for cyclists?
A: Souanyas is nestled in a mountainous region, and while public transport options might be limited directly to the commune, regional train and bus services connect larger towns in the Pyrénées-Orientales department. Cyclists may need to combine public transport with cycling or arrange for local transfers to reach Souanyas and access the routes.

Q: Where can I park my car if I'm cycling around Souanyas?
A: Parking for cyclists is generally available in the village of Souanyas and in the starting points of various routes in nearby towns like Olette or Serdinya. Look for designated parking areas in these communes, especially near public facilities or trailheads, to safely leave your vehicle while you enjoy your ride.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest accessible by road bike from Souanyas?
A: Yes, the Conflent region, where Souanyas is located, is rich in historical and cultural significance. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38701500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villefranche-de-Conflent – Saint-Michel de Cuxa Abbey loop from Olette - Canaveilles-les-Bains&lt;/a&gt; allow you to explore areas with cultural heritage. The region's roads often pass through picturesque villages and offer glimpses of ancient architecture and historical sites.
tips:
A gradual climb on the good paved road! It was climbed during the 15th stage of the Tour de France in 2021!

To do out of season because the road can be very busy (even if there is a deflection on the side)

The coll de la Llosa, with an altitude of 1866 meters, is a mountain pass that separates the plateau from the Quillane pass with the Capcir region, north of the Tossa peak.
On this side it is very simple and fast, but from it you can start countless loops.
